content.question: If the 6/8-year tracks were abolished or significantly reformed, what would be the effect on enrollment composition and achievement segregation in the remaining 4-year gimnázium and general secondary (szakgimnázium) pathways, given that free school choice and selective commuting would remain legally protected?
content.reduction_path: Model enrollment shifts under counterfactual scenarios (e.g., 6/8-year tracks converted to mixed-ability primary schools) using Hungarian administrative education data; analyze whether middle-class families would concentrate in remaining selective schools or increase use of private schools and church schools, and project changes in overall system segregation.
